sections.section: Current Ranking – is Forondil S-Tier , A-Tier , or D-Tier ?!
sections.content:
See how Forondil is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .

Forondil is the first flying hero with cavalry and this is obviously his case, most of his kit resolves around cavalry so make sure to take cavalry with him and if you are playing the Springwarden faction you have to run the flying cavalry they have and nothing on the field will be save from you.

Theia is a really good pair with Forondil as both are flying heroes, have a lot of synergy and with Theia you have the shielding and survivability aspect that cavalry naturally misses a bit (See full build here ).

Bakshi is the pairing that I had the most fun with as they are just so nuking everything and with Bakshi you also have HP buffs to make the march more durable. In this case, however, I recommend you to run Bakshi as the primary commander (See full build here ).

Theodore is obviously also a good pick as they got released along each other, both are about cavalry and have solid synergy and I believe they will make a huge impact towards the later stages of the Season (See full build here ).

Emrys works and deals aton of damage with Forondil but the main issue I have is that he requires to be actively attacked to trigger the extra rage and often enough you’ll be hunting players that run away so you won’t make much use of that (See full build here ).

Ffraegar might be a little odd to you in the list but she has nice buffs and is a flying hero as well so if you’re not able to use the ones above she can work okay-ish with Forondil (See full build here ).

Hosk doesn’t offer too myn synergies which might sound odd as he is so versatile, but in this case it was just lackluster in my tests (See full build here ).

Kregg is a flying hero but for archers and simply pairing them is only if you’re in desperate need of another flying hero for you leagion (See full build here ).

Alistair only real synergy here is that they are both cavalry heroes but I didn’t had much fun with them at all (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Oath of Stormpeak is an amazing artifact for Forondil as you have great stats, great damage and the execute effect is pretty much all you’re looking for even when the enemy is running away (and with the march speed they don’t stand a chance anyway). Kingslayer is an amazing artifact for Forondil as you have great stats, great damage and the execute effect is pretty much all you’re looking for even when the enemy is running away (and with the march speed they don’t stand a chance anyway).

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Forondil with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Forondil that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. The most modern talent build here with Forondil where you really want to go into the control tree to get the sweet talents there as much as possible. You can adjust the final nodes in the control tree as you like them, they are free for mix and match, just make sure you get the main nodes as recommended. Honestly, a more outdated build here wiwth him that used to be a lot more popular around the time when he was released and actually I wouldn’t run it in the current meta anymore, like at all.

I hope this guide for building Forondil helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. Thanks for reading and have a great day.

sections.content:
See how Theodore is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .

I already stated above that it makes very little sense to use him as a garrison commander so rather focus on running him with cavalry in the wild and maraud around, this is what he work for even better.

Emrys is a good pairing for the simple fact that he has a crazy damage output but struggles with survivability and with Theodore you have that plus you reduce the target HP which helps Emrys deal his damage to the very end quickly (See full build here ).

Forondil also works fine but I feel you rather want to set him up with a hero that enables his skills more than Theodore does and I would absolutely run Forondil as the primary legion commander in that case (See full build here ).

Bakshi with Theodore (no matter which hero you set as the legion commander) is a very sturdy and tanky cavalry legion and although they go well together on paper, I personally find that tanky cavalry is somewhat weird and goes against the archetype. But they work (See full build here ).

Alistair is just a backup if you don’t have any other options as unless they are both about cavalry there is not too much synergy (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Kingslayer is only viable IF you do use Theodore as the garrison commander and this is a great IF. I wouldn’t recommend doing that but if you do, you want to use this artifact. Breath of the Forest is only viable IF you do use Theodore as the garrison commander and this is a great IF. I wouldn’t recommend doing that but if you do, you want to use this artifact.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Theodore with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Theodore that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. Here we have the, in general, most useful talent tree build that you will use for every situation unless garrison Theodore. You want to make use of the Skill Tree here as the Skill Tree is simply the strongest tree of all and in many situations I really would run Theodore primary just to make use of that tree unless there are other important reasons. The talents there are simply too good and if you follow my recommendation you will have as much fun with Theodore as possible. This is the Garriosn build in case you want to station him there. Again, I pointed that out multiple times that i don#t recommend that too much as there are simply better heroes for that but if you don#t have them and want to run Theodore, this is for sure the build I recommend you to use.

I hope this guide for building Theodore helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. Thanks for reading and have a great day.

sections.section: Current Ranking – is Ffraegar S-Tier , A-Tier , or D-Tier ?!
sections.content:
See how Ffraegar is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .

There’s two ways to use Ffraegar – one is to run her along another flying hero and use flying units which is a crazy hunting setup. The other is to pair her with any bufer hero like Hosk and run with marksman units.

Syndrion is, obviously, a perfect pairing as they are released together, they are both marksman and overall all their skills and talents just go hand-in-hand. At the end I would recommend you to to use Syndrion as the primary commander in this march, though (See full build here ).

Maggrat with the way to scale normal attacks and crits work really well with her (See full build here ).

Zayda with the way to scale normal attacks and crits work really well with her (See full build here ).

Hosk is a phenomenal pair for Ffraegar as he can keep up with the very long range and buff up the damage output and with all the crits happening in Ffraegar’s skills so they seem to be a killer combo (See full build here ).

Kinnara is also a excellent markman hero you can pair with Ffraegar and get a ton of synergy with and go pretty crazy as well (See full build here ).

Nico seems like a perfect match as he is a marksman hero, too but actually I more list him here to warn you that his active skills has madium range (so you force Ffraegar with her amazing range into closer combat) and you can’t form a flying march. You might use it as basic march with marksman but I wouldn’t recommend it anywhere beyond that (See full build here ).

Theia is another natural pairing with Ffraegar as both are flyer heroes (you might not notice this right off the bat but Ffraegar is a flyer unit hero) – Theia will work with the long-range of Ffraegar’s active skill and make the whole march that is full of damage dealing a lot more sustain (See full build here ).

Kregg can be a viable early pair and although he is a flying hero, he only cares very little about flying units and you can still get away with that pair (See full build here ).

Gwanwyn can also work but away from both being markman heroes there is not a ton of synergy but if that’s your only possible pair you still do it (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Goldcrest is an amazing marksman artifact with Ffraegar and you will deal incredible damage doing so + the overall Marksman unit buff is also out of this world, too. Shadowblades is an amazing marksman artifact with Ffraegar and you will deal incredible damage doing so + the overall Marksman unit buff is also out of this world, too.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Ffraegar with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Ffraegar that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. This tree is universally for all pairings when using Ffraegar along flying units to fly above terrain and be very mobile. Ideally you want to use her with the Wilderburg flying units and their special skill, but it also works well with all other flying units. When you pair Ffraegar with Syndrion and/or are out there to hunt down mage legions, this is the ideal talent tree to use afor that.

I hope this guide for building Ffraegar helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. Thanks for reading and have a great day.

sections.content:

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .
With Syndrion 3rd skill and the way his kit works, you only want to use him with marksman units all together.

Ffraegar also boosts normal attack crit rate and damage and works well with Syndrion and is the absolute best pairing you can run here for marksman and even on the top in general. They both enhance each other so much and if you run their artifact you will give everyone a hard time (See full build here ).

Maggrat with the way to scale normal attacks and crits work really well with her (See full build here ).

Zayda with the way to scale normal attacks and crits work really well with her (See full build here ).

Hosk is one of the important pairings for Syndrion as you have the extra attack that hits hard so you want to boost normal attack damage, crit and damage output as much as possible – and as Hosk does that well along being quite a universal hero he works great with Syndrion (See full build here ).

Kinnara is a possible pairing as well although I don’t like it as much. Most synergy here is Kinnara apply DEF break so Syndrion deals more normal attack damage. Not bad but also not outstanding as the ones above (See full build here ).

Gwanwyn has a lot of synergy with Syndrion as she will apply Poison with each normal attack but switching targets is not perfect and she is overall not the greatest hero. Still, if you are building syndrion for your 2nd marksman march and have Nico Kinnara already in use, this is a viable option but just not slamming it (See full build here ).

Nico is a suboptimal pairing and they don’t have much synergy away from the fact that Nico is also a marksman hero (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Goldcrest is an amazing marksman artifact with Syndrion and you will deal incredible damage doing so + the overall Marksman unit buff is also out of this world, too. My personal favorite one to use with Syndrion if you don’t run him with Ffraegar or don’t have Goldcrest availabe. Shadowblades is an amazing marksman artifact with Syndrion and you will deal incredible damage doing so + the overall Marksman unit buff is also out of this world, too. My personal favorite one to use with Syndrion if you don’t run him with Ffraegar or don’t have Goldcrest availabe.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Syndrion with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Syndrion that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. This is the ideal talent build that makes him put out consistent normal damage at the highest rate, ideally you want to pair him with Ffraegar for this.

I hope this guide for building Syndrion helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. sections.section: Current Ranking – is Liliya S-Tier , A-Tier , or D-Tier ?!
sections.content:
See how Liliya is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .

Use all Magic or at least as many as possible as she will buff up HP and attack of all magic units in her legion through her 3rd skill and some of the talent points shown below will also only affect Magic Units exclusively. It’s not required but it will help a lot and make your life easier at the end of the day.

Velyn works incredibly well with Liliya in the open field as both are all about Magic Units and all buffs and debuffs he brings will apply to Liliya as well and with the reduced march speed of enemy legions, you will lock them down and finish them off really effectively (See full build here ).

Waldyr has a lot of magic damage synergy with Liliya and if you don’t have Velyn built well but progressed well with Waldyr, they both can work really well along each other in a legion (See full build here ).

Hosk is one of the versatile commanders in Call of Dragons and very versatile to be used along Liliya as well and she will benefit a lot from the buffs he provides through his 1st and 3rd skill (See full build here ).

Theia will provide your legion with a shield and although you will not get all of her skill buffs active, she makes your Liliya legion a lot more durable and as Liliya already deals good damage it can work well (See full build here ).

Bertrand can work but there is not too much how Bertrand can get his marks here to blow off and the combination is just overall not designed to work together unless it’s a real backup one (See full build here ).

Alwyn works in many ways as the epic version of Velyn and helps in the open field by reducing target march speed, dealing damage over time, buffing Magic Units plus dealing a ton of damage. The only downside is, this legion can turn a little bit into a glass-cannon as you don’t have any defensive things going on (See full build here ).

Atheus is also a commander pairing with Liliya for open field combat as you get skill damage, additional rage and he also buffs march speed for all troops (See full build here ).

Indis is the last on the list here and the only reason the work together is that Indis attack range is also very far. A very backup pairing and they don’t stand in each others way (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Phoenix Eye can heal your units up and increases the base stats of Magic Units. This is a helpful artifact to run with Liliya if you run her along another damage dealing hero so you can gain some survivability. Tear of Arbon can heal your units up and increases the base stats of Magic Units. This is a helpful artifact to run with Liliya if you run her along another damage dealing hero so you can gain some survivability.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Liliya with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Liliya that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. This is the talent tree you would use with Liliya in 2024 in like 95% of all cases. Works with all magic heroes you apri her and in PvE and PvP the best. The only exception here is when you use her with Tohar, then you don’t want to use this tree (see below). As mentioned above, this is the talent tree you want to use when you pair her with heroes that apply buff effects (espcially with Tohar but also heroes like Hosk and Theia tend to work better with that tree). These two are the only valid trees for Liliya these days and if you see sources showing other trees, well, they are way outdated mid-2023 so don’t use them anymore.

I hope this guide for building Liliya helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. Thanks for reading and have a great day.

sections.section: Current Ranking – is Kinnara S-Tier , A-Tier , or D-Tier ?!
sections.content:
See how Kinnara is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .

Kinnara is a Marksman Unit commander and although the talent build I recommend doesn’t invest in the marksman Tree, some of her buffs only apply to Marksman Units so it makes sense that you take her with a full marksman Unit legion to the field to make sure you get the most consistent performance.

Nico as primary commander with Kinnara secondary makes a lot of sense since Nico also has a huge target debuff in his active skill. So, either Kinnara or Nico will use their skill and debuff so the target legion takes a lot more damage and then the other one hits with the active skill (See full build here ).

Ffraegar is also a marksman combination that has a lot of synergy and can get you a lot of value (See full build here ).

Hosk is a exensive but yet also powerful combination, espcially when you have him expertised so you get the critical bonus applied to the target very frequently through their kit. I run Hosk secondary here as I really like the talents that Kinnara has a lot more (See full build here ).

Syndrion is like Kinnara a marksman hero but their overall synergy is not above that they are about marksman and their kits work really differently to make this a top-priority pairing (See full build here ).

Kregg can be also a pairing you might try out if the above ones will not work for you (and that applies to quite a big number of players). You will gain synergy of 3 of Kregg’s skills, he is ranged as well and he deals area damage which is also nice (See full build here ).

Gwanwyn is also a good option along Kinnara as she is also a Marksman hero and although her 2nd skill is about Peacekeeping and not helpful, her march speed buff of her 3rd skill works really well with Kinnara in the open field (See full build here ).

Theia is a more of an experimental pairing right now but I actually liked it, espcially as she will boost the next rage skill with her active skill and that has no time limit, so Kinnara can use her hard-hitting active skill and deal significantly more damage + the shielding and survivability works (See full build here ).

Eliana might be last call here if you don’t have any of the above options available. She provides survivability but that’s about it and there’s no reason to run this unless you have no other option (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Shadowblades is a newer artifact and as it synergizes with DEF Break that Kinnara can apply, there is a strong synergy and along Shadowblades the strongest artifact choices. Rattle Spear is a newer artifact and as it synergizes with DEF Break that Kinnara can apply, there is a strong synergy and along Shadowblades the strongest artifact choices.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Kinnara with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Kinnara that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. This is her general counterattack damage talent build for the highest damage output where you need it with her This here is probably the most viable open field build, utilizing her single-target damage but also apply debuffs and silence to be as annoying to deal with as possible in all PvP situations.

I hope this guide for building Kinnara helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. Thanks for reading and have a great day.

sections.section: Current Ranking – is Velyn S-Tier , A-Tier , or D-Tier ?!
sections.content:
See how Velyn is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .
With his kit, even when you use a talent build that doesn’t use the magic Tree at all, you want to bring Magic Units as his 3rd and 4th skill are strong buffs for Magic Units.

Liliya is about fire but her kit is similar to Velyn and together they gain the Magic Unit crit, deal crazy damage and even with both heroes at 5111 you have such a pack running that makes so much fun to play. You can either run Velyn primary for more control effects or Liliya for more active damage dealt – or just use the one that has more skills leveled (See full build here ).

Waldyr is not just a cheap synergy hero to pair Velyn with, Waldyr also has freeze effect and buffs Magic Units and once you expertise Velyn you get that 400 damage factor extra damage all the time and have one of the nastiest legions to deal with running. Yes, Waldyr is ‘only’ an epic hero but with Velyn primary and Waldyr secondary you have one of the most devestating PvP combos running (See full build here ).

Alwyn is the best F2P hero in Call of Dragons and goes so well with Velyn and is a little bit a epic version of him, ticking damage over time but also providing a ton of synergy (See full build here ).

Theia deals ranged magic damage so you can run her with Velyn and she adds a ton of survivability. I’d prefer the pairings above at any time but if you have her as the only option available it’s working (See full build here ).

Atheus deals ranged magic damage and although it’s ‘only’ single-target damage, his 2nd skill with extra rage generation and the control tree on Velyn makes them a pretty beast combo (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Phoenix Eye can heal your units up and increases the base stats of Magic Units. This is a helpful artifact to run with Velyn if you run him along another damage dealing hero so you can gain some survivability. Not my top-tier choice but can work if that’s your best option available. Tear of Arbon can heal your units up and increases the base stats of Magic Units. This is a helpful artifact to run with Velyn if you run him along another damage dealing hero so you can gain some survivability. Not my top-tier choice but can work if that’s your best option available.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Velyn with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Velyn that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. Do not go for those sources that give you like a dozen different talent builds for Velyn – this was a thing of mid-2023 when he was released and I am flabbergasted by the amount of outdated sources still being around. This here is the only useful and working talent tree you need in 2024 with Velyn and it works for everything (except maybe group-PvP-mass-battles that happen on new servers here and there but you don’t actually want to participate in them anyway). For everything else, Velyn is a great and versatile hero and the control tree is amazing if you make use of it like shown here.

I hope this guide for building Velyn helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. If you liked it or you further questions (or maybe also annnotations that you believe should be added here in this guide), please don’t hesitate to post them below in the comment section. Thanks for reading and have a great day.

sections.section: Current Ranking – is Waldyr S-Tier , A-Tier , or D-Tier ?!
sections.content:
See how Waldyr is ranking in the different game modes of Call of Dragons in my current tier list here and also make sure that you’re running the best faction in Call of Dragons or you might really affect your progress negatively – I have an updated guide on the best factions in Call of Dragons here

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .
You really want to run purely Magic in his legion as his skills and also some talents that only affect Magic untis so don#t over-complicate things with him and bring them exclusively.

Velyn as the primary hero makes a lot of sense with Waldyr for the reason as you then can use the Control Tree and he’s overall stronger. However, if your Waldyr is maxed and you haven#t worked much on Velyn you might consider running Waldyr as the primary hero in the legion. Both have great hero skill damage and slow the target so the legion you target will really have a hard time getting away (See full build here ).

Liliya is another commander that does really well with Walkdyr and you might even consider using Waldyr as the primary hero, although be aware that many players tend to focus down epic heroes when they see them in the open field. But both together will deal a heckload of damage (See full build here ).

Alwyn works as there is synerggy for the debuffing and if you don’t have the legendary magic heroes, this is probably your best bet (See full build here ).

Atheus can be a great options as well as you get good hero skill damage plus he generates more rage so you can run a skill build for higher damage and still get the extra rage to cycle through your active skill more frequently (See full build here ).

Theia provides shield and can provide your Waldyr with some durability. Not the craziest or best pairing but one you can use (See full build here ).

Without over-complicating things, you want to run Emrys with a full Cavalry legion for the simply fact that several talent builds only affect cavalry units and his scaling 3rd skill is also only buffing cavalry units + you want to make use of the quick cavalry units in the open field when hunting down your enemies.

Bakshi has amazing buffs for cavalry and you can easily run a 5111 Bakshi along Emrys for an incredible cavarly leagion. They are a basic ambush army but require some investment to be viable but youc an run around the open field and easily ambush other players’ marches with that combo (See full build here ).

Hosk is a good pairing although the investment is quite high here – but you gain a ton of buffs, survivability, damage and also legion capacity so you can use this quick interception setup to run and tank away any forces that are incoming. Not too interesting for the average player but for spenders a valid one so I have it not listed on top although I beleive that might be strongest combo with Emrys on paper (See full build here ).

Theodore give the crazy damage-dealing kit of Emrys some good sustain as this is what he struggles with the most (See full build here ).

Alistair is a good pairing, deals damage himself that is alright but also has more rage accumulation in his skill kit and as soon as Emrys is expertised, Emrys will turn that rage accumulation instantly into 100 rage. They will just spam their active skills a lot and have great buffs together. Great pairing to intercept flag construction OR enemies tring to destroy one of your flags (See full build here ).

Forondil can work as well but there is one big part that will have a negative impact and that is that they seem like the top damage-dealing pair but Emrys needs to be hit to trigger and with Forondil you will most likely hunt legions fleeing from you (See full build here ).

Theia along the Springwardens as your faction can do scary things as you suddenly get Theia’s 3rd skill buff and shielding along with the damage and mobility from Emrys. Not the dream team as Emrys boosts skill damage and Theia doesn’t deal any skill damage. But, again, only interesting to you if your have the Eagles of the Springwarden faction (See full build here ).

Eliana is more like a budget pairing with Emrys, she adds survivability whcih can sometimes be an issue you run into with cavalry (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Sorland’s Blade will buff your cavalry a lot plus you can teleport anywhere, hunting down targets has never been that deadly and along the march speed you have with Emrys legion, there’s no getting away. This is THE ambush open-field artifact with Emrys. Storm Arrows will buff your cavalry a lot plus you can teleport anywhere, hunting down targets has never been that deadly and along the march speed you have with Emrys legion, there’s no getting away. Theia doesn’t care about troop types at all and if you run her in the open field, you pair her as the secondary hero so take whatever troops type your primary hero requires. Later when you expertised her, you run her in a supportive legion so troops types aren’t that important there either. The only exception is if you pair her with a flying hero like Kregg or Atheus, then you want to use flying units.

Ffraegar is, in my humble opinion, the best pairing with Theia as you can make use of Ffraegars logn range skill and Theia adds a lot of sustain to the damage-heavy kit of Ffraegar (See full build here ).

Forondil also works incredibly well with Theia as you can make use of their flying capabilities, the whole synergy is there and also vacalry just struggles with survivability which Theia bumps up a lot (See full build here ).

Atheus is also a flying unit that you can combine well with Theia and flying units if your faction is league of Order as their special flying are pretty strong and with the support build below you will get an incredibly strong pairing as Atheus will help generate more rage constantly. I recommend running Theia as the primary hero unless you have your Atheus a lot further upgraded (See full build here ).

Kregg is the other flying hero in the game have great cross-synergies and buff each other up. They are a mutual pairing and you will start using Kregg as primary hero unless you have Theia leveled up higher. You should be Wilderburg to use the special flying unit (See full build here ).

Madeline is a very tanky legion with Theia, especially as Madeline will be able to trigger the buff from Theias’s passive skill that triggers each time the legion gets a shield. Not a pairing for the average player, though (See full build here ).

Liliya also works well with Theia even though you cannot really make much use of the flying aspect (See full build here ).

Nika is a great pairing, too as you will gain the shielding passive buff and then Nika deals her massive active skill damage (if you’re looking for a non-flying combo) (See full build here ).

Visage of the Sanctus if you run Theia primary with marksman, choose Shadowblades, with cavalry you want Sorlands Blade, with magic units for with the Infernal Flame and with infantry choose Dragonrift. This isdepending what you run her with. Troop Type Dependant if you run Theia primary with marksman, choose Shadowblades, with cavalry you want Sorlands Blade, with magic units for with the Infernal Flame and with infantry choose Dragonrift. This isdepending what you run her with.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Theia with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Alternatively you can consider running Kinnara primary to be able to have the Control Tree active but you want Nico expertised as you can use Ambush in the Control Tree to practically have always the chance to inflight DEF break and then Nico will deal his 200 extra damage (See full build here ).

Hosk works great (but you can actually pair him with any hero in Call of Dragons). I only say he works fine as he is only available for high-spender sin the earlier stages of Call of Dragons as you can only get him through the Honour Store with VIP Level 10+. He works amazing as he buffs up practically everything that Nico does but requires, as I said, quite a high investment including his 3rd and 4th skill leveled up. Theia is a valid option BUT ONLY if you are using Wilderburg as your faction to have the flying riders that will trigger Theia’s 3rd skill. If you do NOT run that faction, don’t think about pairing Theia along Nico! (See full build here ).

Ffraegar works and has a lot of synergy but there is one big issue that I don’t like here and that is the different ranges and Nico forces Ffraegar with the very long range into medium range which I do not appreciate that much and see way better cases for. Away from that and the way their kits work, they still go well with each other (See full build here ).

Gwanwyn is a great early-game pairing as you can easily level her up, they share the marksman syernergy and just go really well along each other. The further you upgrade Nico, you want to use him as the primary hero for the legion. With the added damage against Darklings and Dark Legions this is more an early-game pairing, though (See full build here ).

Kregg is also a really nice F2P pairing with Nico, also about Marksmen and the area damage. You’re missing out on the flying units synergy unless you run Wilderberg as your faction. If you do, I’d prefer Kregg over Gwanwyn anytime (See full build here ).

Syndrion has only the synergy that both do run and work with marksman and there is actually no deeper synergy (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Shadowblades works also great with Nico but that is because it’s an amazing marksman artifact all round. Still, In the global terms I wouldn’t recommend it and I see better options for this artifact but when you just look at Nico it’s an incredible choice. Rattle Spear works also great with Nico but that is because it’s an amazing marksman artifact all round. Still, In the global terms I wouldn’t recommend it and I see better options for this artifact but when you just look at Nico it’s an incredible choice.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Nico with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Nico that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. Let me get a few things here out of the way before we go into the talent trees. You see below the talent trees that work with Nico, also in later seasons. Many, almost all, sources out there that have talent trees created them in Spring 2024 when Call of Dragons was released and never touched them again. But season skills, additional heroes and the meta changed coming along with it. So, the below trees are the ones working late 2024 and if you see other talent trees out there, they are outdated… So, this first talent tree here is for boosting marksmen normal attack damage with any other marksmen hero that is not using skill damage primarily and working quite well in all parts where you would use Nico. This talent tree here is used best with marksmen T4 troops along the usual marksment hero secondary pairs to counter and hunt down mage legions. This talent build is primarily for PvE content, in particular for raiding Behemoths where this will help you put out as damage as possible.

sections.content:

Important! Don’t miss out on free Gems, Keys, Boosters and Speedups and frequently check my list of active gift codes for Call of Dragons here .
Combine Kella with gathering units as gathering is the only real thing you will do with Kella in Call of Dragons.
Pair her with whatever you have and feel like, ideally a hero that gives her the most load space.

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Greenfinger Sickle increases gather speed very well and can spawn a gather node ofr you when you use the active ability. Lucia’s Horn increases gather speed very well and can spawn a gather node ofr you when you use the active ability.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Kella with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Kella that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. The only talent build that makes sense with kella is the one that maximizes her gathering abilities. Unlock the center node and then go right into the Gathering Tree for getting EXP for gathering which helps a lot and Veteran Goldsmith to increaes Gold Gather Speed with Kella even further. Overall Load Capacity helps to carry more resources while gathering and Joyful Burdern even more, this helps a lot if you don’t send Kella out with a secondary deputy to gather within alliance territory where risk is small that you get attacked when you’re offline while gahtering. With War Banner you get more legion capacity and Leeway helps when you’re offline so your legion can get away if they get attacked while you gather + Joyful Labor helps there too. Between Windfall and Lay of the Land I’d take Lay of the Land as the boosted resource production is nice and boosts your resources automatically without anything you need to do. Last points now go into the Overall Tree for more march speed and War Banner for more legion capacity and last but not least into Logistics Master for the last gather speed increase.

I hope this guide for building Kella helped you investing without wasting much time and resources. Nico with his 2nd skill that boosts engineering is a good combination if you do some engineering as both will increase building speed (See full build here ).

Kregg is also boosting engineering and can be a great hero if you have his 4th skill unlocked (and even better, when upgraded) (See full build here ).

Let’s now take a look at the numerous artifacts that you can possible use. We have tested many of them and some have amazing synergy and others are more in for the stats but no matter what artifacts you have possibly available, you will find the one that works best. Please also check out the full tier list of all artifacts here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Joyous Fireworks for gathering works quite well as it will increase your load capacity and you can make an initial boost of gathering. Greenfinger Sickle for gathering works quite well as it will increase your load capacity and you can make an initial boost of gathering.

With the new war pet system in Call of Dragons it’s aso important to pair Ordo with the right pet to gain more synergies. Please also check out the full tier list of all war pets in Call of Dragons here to see which ones are worth the invest in general.

Below you will find the talent tree builds for Ordo that make sense when running as the primary commander in any legion in Call of Dragons. Please also refer to the description below each talent tree as I explain a general route on how you should spend your talents there + some additional background for each talent build that I very likely will appreciate. As Ordo doesn’t have the gathering tree, which is odd as he is your mana gathering hero in Call of Dragons, going into the Engineering Tree is the only useful option with Ordo to get as much engineering speed to help building your alliance structures as well as possible. After you unlocked the core node of Ordo, go directly into the Engineering Tree and pick up Fortification to boost building speed even further and Havoc in case you send him as support to destroy other alliances structures. From here take Engineering Models as building makes a lot more sense with Orda than destructing and in the second half of the Engineering Tree take the other Fortification point, Stockpiling for the case you’re destroying enemy alliance buildings, Eminent Domain for a general engineering speed buff and Right to Work. The last points then should be in the Overall Tree with more march speed and War Banner for more legion capacity that will be useful and Overall Speed in the Engineering Tree.

I hope this guide for building Ordo helped you investing without wasting much time and resources.
